images.altText: Donald Trump in a suit and tie speaking outside the Oval Office, holding his hand out in front of him.
A man walking along a rock seawall with large oil tankers lined up in the background.

articles.title: Pope will continue to 'speak out against war' despite Trump's attacks

children.content: Writing on social media after the pope called for peace, US President Donald Trump says the first American pope is "terrible for foreign policy" and he should "stop catering to the Radical Left".

image.altText: A man dressed in white robes stands at a lectern

articles.title: Sailor records tense exchange between the US and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: A tense exchange between the United States and Iran, recorded by a sailor in the Persian Gulf who has been speaking with the ABC, shows the extremely fraught situation in the Strait of Hormuz, as a fragile ceasefire hangs in the balance.

image.altText: Birds flying in the foreground as a large gas tanker is docked at sea with a large city skyline in the background.

articles.title: There's a surprising message from inside Iran after the ceasefire

children.content: While the bombs may have stopped falling on Iran for now, Iranians who've been speaking with ABC News say the mood on the ground is anything but happy.

image.altText: A woman in a hijab walking in front of a wall that has a painting of an aircraft carrier in water being bombed

children.content: The US Navy is expected to begin its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after Trump warned he would do so following failed peace talks with Iran. Follow live.

items.title: US Navy begins blockade of Hormuz, Trump claims Iran 'very badly' wants deal

updates.title: Trump says 'right people' have called and want to make a deal
US-Iran ceasefire is holding, Pakistani PM says
Trump: 'Attack ships' will be 'immediately eliminated' if they enter blockade area

items.title: Trump's defamation lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein story dismissed

image.altText: Rupert Murdoch and Donald Trump on a golf course.

children.content: The case related to a Wall Street Journal article that described a sexually suggestive letter that the newspaper said was included in a 2003 album compiled for Epstein’s 50th birthday.

items.title: 'Should have done this earlier': Former CIA boss backs Trump's blockade

children.content: Donald Trump's looming blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will likely lead to confrontation warns a former 4-star general and former head of the CIA.

image.altText: David sits in a suit, speaking and gesticulating with his hand, holding a pen

items.title: Trump's strait blockade has two immediate implications for the world and China

children.content: Donald Trump's planned United States naval blockade of Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz reveals the president once again playing loose with the fortunes of financial markets and the global economy as he struggles to find a way out of the war that he and Israel started.

image.altText: US President Donald Trump gives a thumbs up.

items.title: The US will cut off the Strait of Hormuz. Here's what the blockade means

children.content: US president Donald Trump has ordered his nation's military to cut off all Iranian ports from maritime traffic passing through the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most vital shipping routes.

image.altText: US president Donald Trump between two ships.

items.title: Social media checks for all visa applicants under Angus Taylor plan

children.content: Social media screening would be introduced for all visa applicants under an immigration policy to be revealed by Opposition leader Angus Taylor.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie sits slumped in a chair, staring across a desk in front of him.

items.title: Booking.com customers involved in possible data and security breach

children.content: Some Booking.com customers are being warned their personal information may have been accessed by unauthorised third parties, prompting fresh concerns about the security of travellers' data.

gallery.altText: A close up of the Booking.com app icon on the screen of a mobile phone.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of the Booking.com app icon on the screen of a mobile phone.

items.title: Albanese names new defence force chief

children.content: Vice Admiral Mark Hammond, who has spent 40 years in the navy, will replace current defence force chief David Johnston when he retires in July.

image.altText: A man sits indoors in a white navy uniform looking at the camera. Behind him hangs a white Australian Navy flag.

items.title: Federal MPs urged not to speak out about looming Ben Roberts-Smith trial

children.content: A federal MP and former commando has urged colleagues from across the aisle to refrain from speaking out about the arrest of accused war criminal Ben Roberts-Smith.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith in a suit and tie

items.title: Coachella boasted big-name artists — the special guests were maybe even bigger

children.content: The world's most talked-about music festival took place over the weekend, boasting a bevy of unexpected highlights.

gallery.altText: Sabrina Carpenter raises arms standing at a microphone in a gold costume, framed by studio curtains at Coachella

items.title: How did a 3km hike go so wrong? Retracing Celine Cremer's final steps

children.content: Belgian backpacker Celine Cremer was on the trip of a lifetime, but on a hike in Tasmania she left the trail and was never seen again. It took a private investigator, a YouTuber, loved ones and an army of volunteers to find answers.

image.altText: Blonde woman with dark blonde wavey hair out, seated at a table. A brunette woman sits beside her, hand  rests on her cheek

items.title: Student vaccinated without consent prompts council concern

children.content: A Tasmanian mayor is calling on the state government to take on responsibility for running school immunisation programs, after a student was vaccinated without caregiver consent.

image.altText: A student receives a measles vaccine injection at the Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne (EPFL).

items.title: Growers could 'pull the pin' as fuel costs soar

children.content: As fuel prices remain high amid the war in the Middle East, growers and fishers are among those having to make tough decisions to remain viable.

image.altText: Vicki holds a fish with tongs and smiles.

items.title: Reptile remains believed to be a crocodile found dumped on side of road

children.content: Reptile remains — which are believed to be from a crocodile — have been collected by authorities after they were found dumped on the side of an Adelaide road.

image.altText: A crocodile in brown water looking straight on at the camera close-up like a portrait.

items.title: Euphoria season 3 cast on past, present and future of hit show

children.content: Season 3 of Euphoria has premiered today as members of the cast reflect on where the show has been and where it's going.

items.title: Australia's best inventor you've never heard of

children.content: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

items.title: These five soup recipes are comfort in a bowl

children.content: Looking for new soup recipes as the weather cools? From minestrone to hot and sour dumplings, we have you covered.

image.altText: A hand hugging a bowl of lentil soup with potato and spinach. On the table are lemons, cumin and sea salt.

items.title: Butters sent to Tribunal over 'abusive and insulting' umpire sledge

children.content: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters will front the AFL Tribunal over an exchange with umpire Nick Foot.

image.altText: Zak Butters reacts during Port's match against St Kilda

items.title: Backyard astronomer who helped to discover 100 distant planets awarded medal

children.content: From a backyard observatory in regional Victoria, Gippsland astronomer Chris Stockdale spends his nights helping confirm distant planets by tracking tiny changes in starlight that reveal new worlds beyond our solar system.

image.altText: A man wearing glasses and a blue button-down shirt, holding a box and grabbing a large telescope

items.title: World-famous Horizontal Falls to allow more tourists, with cultural focus

children.content: Tourism operators, the state government and traditional owners strike an agreement about the future of tourism at the famed Horizontal Falls in WA's Kimberley.

image.altText: A huge set of bush clad sandstone peninsula with a gap in the middle showing ocean rushing through

items.title: Memorial for women allegedly killed by men removed from Melbourne laneway

children.content: The founder of Australian Femicide Watch says she is heartbroken over the removal of the She Matters mural in Hosier Lane dedicated to more than 200 women who have died since January 2025.

image.altText: A person stands at the top of a ladder against a wall with women's pictures as a crowd gathers below.

items.title: 'Absolute garbage': NSW police minister hits back at mayor's resourcing claims

children.content: A $1.8 million plaza closed by council has become a flashpoint in a war of words over police resources in Sydney's west.

image.altText: Chris Minns and Yasmin Catley

items.title: Canada has flipped the script on child care. Could Australia copy?

children.content: Australia definitely won't nationalise child care, even though the data suggests there's a strong case. Perhaps a more feasible idea would be to copy what Canada has done.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: One Nation approaches independent MPs as it makes the case to be SA's opposition

children.content: One Nation SA leader Cory Bernardi has approached independent MPs to make the case for his party — instead of the Liberals — to be recognised as the state's official opposition.

image.altText: composite of Matt Schultz, Lou Nicholson and Geoff Brock

items.title: Health minister and veteran MPs step down before Victorian state election

children.content: Long serving Labor MPs Mary-Anne Thomas, Danny Pearson and Gayle Tierney have announced they will not contest the 2026 state election.

image.altText: Mary Anne Thomas, Danny Pearson, Gayle Tierney

items.title: Two people missing after house destroyed by fire

children.content: A crime scene has been established and two people in their 70s are unaccounted for following a house fire in a remote area of Queensland.

image.altText: Remains of a severely fire damaged home.

items.title: Man agrees he tried to 'humiliate' prominent surfer before fatal assault

children.content: A man has admitted to stripping a prominent Sydney surfer naked and shaking him by his ankles in an attempt to humiliate him before causing his unlawful death.

image.altText: A middle-aged man wearing a cap smiles at the camera.

items.title: Underground 3D printing farms arming Ukraine's soldiers

children.content: In spare rooms and backyard sheds, volunteers across Europe are 3D printing drone bomb parts to supply Ukraine's military.

items.title: Teen who 'weaponised' stolen cars denied bail over risk to community

children.content: A 13-year-old girl is facing court over 109 charges relating to incidents including allegedly driving into a cyclist.

items.title: Hungary's Orbán era ends in a landslide election win for opposition

children.content: After 16 years in power, the powerful far-right leader says he will serve in opposition, with centre-right ⁠candidate Péter Magyar's Tisza Party ​on course for a super majority.

items.title: Rory McIlroy joins exclusive group with second straight Masters victory

children.content: Rory McIlroy becomes just the fourth man in history to win back-to-back Masters titles after holding off a star-studded field in the final round at Augusta.
